name: CENTRIC
drug: Cilengitide 2000 mg IV twice weekly + RT/TMZ; cilengitide for up to 18 months; maintenance TMZ for up to 6 cycles
mechanism: Selective αvβ3/αvβ5 integrin inhibitor; cyclic RGD pentapeptide blocking integrin-mediated cell adhesion
hypothesis: Adding cilengitide to standard RT/TMZ would extend OS in MGMT-methylated newly diagnosed GBM
sponsor_reason: Did not improve outcomes; cilengitide will not be further developed as an anticancer drug; integrins remain a potential treatment target for GBM
analysis: Phase 2 → Phase 3 effect-size collapse: HR 1.02 with identical median OS suggests Phase 2 signal was an artifact of single-arm design vs historical controls. Mechanism-disease fit questionable. Trial design itself was strong (biomarker-enriched, OS endpoint, adequate power).
notes: Companion CORE Phase 2 trial in MGMT-unmethylated patients showed a nominally positive Phase 2 OS signal that did not translate (see CORE row). Chinot commentary in same Lancet Oncol issue (PMID 25163907) provides independent expert reading.

name: CORE
drug: Cilengitide 2000 mg IV 2x/week (standard) OR 5x/week (intensive) + RT/TMZ
mechanism: Selective αvβ3/αvβ5 integrin inhibitor
phase2_evidence: this IS the Phase 2 (preceded by uncontrolled Phase 1/2)
hypothesis: Cilengitide added to RT/TMZ would improve OS in MGMT-unmethylated newly diagnosed GBM (the population with worst prognosis on standard therapy)
sponsor_reason: Standard and intensive cilengitide dose regimens were well tolerated. Inconsistent OS and PFS outcomes and a limited sample size did not allow firm conclusions regarding clinical efficacy in this exploratory phase II study.
analysis: PERFECT case study of Phase 2 → Phase 3 effect-size collapse. Standard cilengitide arm in CORE Phase 2 (MGMT-unmethylated) showed nominally significant OS benefit (HR 0.686, p=0.032). CENTRIC Phase 3 (MGMT-methylated, HR 1.02 p=0.86) was completely flat. Even more telling — within CORE itself, the intensive cilengitide arm (HR 0.858, p=0.377) was non-significant. If the drug worked, more drug should work better, not worse. The non-monotonic dose-response was a Phase 2 red flag the program ignored.
notes: Companion to CENTRIC. Demonstrates that even when a Phase 2 hits its OS endpoint, the Phase 3 can fail. Editorial in same Neuro-Oncology issue: Mason 2015 'End of the road: confounding results of the CORE trial terminate the arduous journey of cilengitide for glioblastoma'.

name: AVAglio
drug: Bevacizumab 10 mg/kg IV q2w with concurrent RT/TMZ and maintenance TMZ; then 15 mg/kg q3w monotherapy until progression
mechanism: Humanized monoclonal antibody binding VEGF; blocks VEGF-receptor interaction and tumor angiogenesis
hypothesis: Adding bevacizumab to RT/TMZ would improve OS and/or PFS in newly diagnosed GBM
sponsor_reason: OS not improved; PFS improved and baseline HRQoL/PS maintained; AE rate higher with bevacizumab. Sponsor framed PFS win + QoL maintenance as clinically meaningful even absent OS benefit.
analysis: Pseudoresponse: bevacizumab reduces contrast enhancement on MRI without affecting tumor biology, inflating PFS. 1-year OS separation (72.4% vs 66.3%) vanishes by 2 years. HRQoL framework chosen over objective neurocognition; RTOG 0825 found cognitive decline using objective testing. Asymmetric alpha allocation (1% PFS, 4% OS).
notes: Same NEJM issue as RTOG 0825 (Feb 20, 2014). Sandmann 2015 (JCO) post-hoc analysis suggested proneural subtype may benefit; not prospectively validated.

name: RTOG 0825
drug: Bevacizumab 10 mg/kg IV q2w + RT/TMZ; placebo arm could cross over to bevacizumab at progression
mechanism: Humanized monoclonal antibody binding VEGF
biomarker: none (all-comers; MGMT and 9-gene signature analyzed for stratification)
phase2_evidence: same Phase 2 evidence base as AVAglio
hypothesis: Adding bevacizumab to RT/TMZ would improve OS and/or PFS in newly diagnosed GBM
sponsor_reason: Bevacizumab remains an important therapy for GBM, but results do not support upfront use. Direct conclusion with no asset-preservation language.
analysis: Replicates AVAglio's OS-negative finding with independent sponsor. Objective neurocognitive testing showed bev decline; AVAglio's HRQoL questionnaires showed maintenance — methodological choice flips QoL conclusion. Best-prognosis subgroup (MGMT-meth + favorable 9-gene) had WORSE OS with bev (15.7 vs 25 mo, p=0.08) — possible harm signal. 41% crossover dilutes OS comparison.
notes: Same NEJM issue as AVAglio. Subsequent NRG paper (PMC8661434) confirmed cognitive decline pattern.

name: ACT IV
drug: Rindopepimut 500 μg + GM-CSF 150 μg intradermally + standard maintenance TMZ
mechanism: EGFRvIII-targeted peptide vaccine; EGFRvIII peptide conjugated to keyhole limpet hemocyanin
biomarker: EGFRvIII+ AND minimal residual disease (<2 cm² post-chemoradiation)
phase2_evidence: 3 single-arm Phase 2 trials (n=105 total) vs matched historical controls
hypothesis: Adding rindopepimut to TMZ would improve OS in EGFRvIII+ newly diagnosed GBM with minimal residual disease. Powering: HR ≤0.71 with 80% power, alpha=0.05.
sponsor_reason: Rindopepimut did not increase survival in patients with newly diagnosed glioblastoma. Combination approaches potentially including rindopepimut might be required to show efficacy of immunotherapy in glioblastoma. Trial stopped early for futility at preplanned interim analysis.
analysis: TEXTBOOK example of historical-control failure. Phase 2 showed mOS 20-22 mo vs historical 16 mo — looked like a 4-mo benefit. But ACT IV control arm reached 20.0 mo, identical to rindopepimut. The 'historical 16-mo benchmark' was outdated; by 2012-2014, MRD-selected EGFRvIII+ patients on standard chemoradiation routinely achieved 17-20 mo. The drug had no real effect; the apparent Phase 2 benefit was control-arm drift over time. Counterintuitive 2-year OS finding in non-MRD population (30% vs 19%, p=0.029) is likely noise and not biologically plausible — vaccines should work less, not more, with greater tumor burden. ITT mOS: 17.4 vs 17.4 mo, HR 0.89, p=0.22.
notes: Stratified by RPA class, MGMT methylation, and geographic region. Useful pairing: ReACT (Phase 2 in recurrent EGFRvIII+ GBM, rindopepimut + bev) showed apparent benefit (mOS 11.3 vs 9.3 mo, HR 0.53) but small (n=70). Editorial: 'EGFRvIII vaccine in glioblastoma — InACT-IVe or not ReACTive enough?' (PMC5737084).

name: CheckMate-143
drug: Nivolumab 3 mg/kg IV q2w until progression or unacceptable toxicity
mechanism: Fully human IgG4 monoclonal antibody inhibiting PD-1 receptor (immune checkpoint inhibitor)
biomarker: none (MGMT analyzed for stratification: ~23% methylated, ~34% unmethylated)
phase2_evidence: nivolumab efficacy in other tumors; limited pre-Phase 3 GBM data
hypothesis: Nivolumab monotherapy would improve OS vs bevacizumab in first-recurrence GBM
sponsor_reason: Did not meet primary endpoint of OS. Patients with methylated MGMT promoter and no baseline corticosteroid use may potentially derive benefit from immune checkpoint inhibition. Safety profile consistent with other tumor types.
analysis: First Phase 3 of immune checkpoint inhibition in GBM. Failed against active comparator (bev, itself OS-negative in newly dx). Likely contributors: (1) GBM is 'cold' tumor — low TMB, low T-cell infiltration; (2) blood-brain barrier limits drug penetration; (3) high baseline corticosteroid use (40% on steroids ≥4 mg/day in 14-15%) blunts immune response; (4) active comparator with its own PFS effects creates tough comparison. Asset-preservation language ('may potentially derive benefit from') points at post-hoc subgroup. CheckMate-498 (newly dx MGMT-unmeth) and -548 (MGMT-meth) both also failed.
notes: 12-mo OS: 42% both arms. ORR higher with bev (23%); duration of response longer with nivo (11.1 vs 5.3 mo) but only 8% responded. Co-published commentary 'Stalemate or new game strategy' (PMID 32437509) and JAMA reevaluation editorial.

name: INTELLANCE-1
drug: Depatuxizumab mafodotin (depatux-m / ABT-414) IV + RT/TMZ + adjuvant TMZ; protocol-specified prophylactic + supportive measures for corneal epitheliopathy
mechanism: Antibody-drug conjugate; mAb806 (anti-EGFR antibody recognizing tumor-specific EGFR epitope) linked to monomethyl auristatin F (microtubule inhibitor)
biomarker: EGFR-amplified (centrally confirmed; ~50% of newly dx GBM)
phase2_evidence: INTELLANCE-2 (Phase 2 recurrent EGFR-amp): HR 0.71 (95% CI 0.50-1.02, p=0.06) — borderline
hypothesis: Adding depatux-m to RT/TMZ would improve OS in EGFR-amplified newly diagnosed GBM. Powering: HR ≤0.75 with 85% power, alpha 2.5% one-sided.
sponsor_reason: Did not meet primary endpoint of OS at interim analysis; demonstrated no survival benefit. Independent Data Monitoring Committee recommended stopping. AbbVie halted enrollment in all ongoing depatux-m studies.
analysis: Biomarker-enriched (EGFR-amplified) but still failed — reinforces broader pattern that EGFR-targeting strategies (rindopepimut vaccine in ACT IV, depatux-m ADC here) consistently fail in GBM despite EGFR being most-studied target. Possible contributors: EGFR amplification in GBM may not be a true driver dependency the way it is in NSCLC; corneal toxicity may have led to dose reductions; INTELLANCE-2 Phase 2 result (HR 0.71, p=0.06) was already borderline — the program proceeded to Phase 3 on a barely-significant Phase 2 trend.
notes: Halted at preplanned interim analysis 2019. Full results published Lassman 2022 Neuro-Oncology. INTELLANCE-2 (recurrent setting, Phase 2) is the related Phase 2 — also failed primary endpoint.

name: EORTC 26101
drug: Lomustine 110 mg/m² oral + bevacizumab 10 mg/kg IV q2w
mechanism: VEGF mAb (anti-angiogenic) added to alkylating chemotherapy
hypothesis: Adding bevacizumab to lomustine would improve OS in recurrent GBM
sponsor_reason: Combination did not provide a survival advantage. PFS extended but at cost of higher toxicity. HRQoL and neurocognitive function unaffected by combination.
analysis: Replicates the AVAglio/RTOG 0825 PFS-positive/OS-negative pattern in the recurrent setting. AEs grade 3-5: 63.6% (combo) vs 38.1% (mono) — substantial toxicity for no survival benefit. Reinforces drug-class conclusion: bevacizumab in GBM produces imaging/PFS effects without survival effects, regardless of setting (newly dx vs recurrent) or combination partner.
notes: Often referenced as the definitive recurrent-GBM bevacizumab trial. MGMT was prognostic but not predictive of bev benefit. NEJM 2017.

name: INTELLANCE-2
drug: Depatux-m 1.25 or 1.0 mg/kg IV q2w + TMZ; OR depatux-m monotherapy
mechanism: Anti-EGFR ADC (mAb806 + MMAF)
hypothesis: Depatux-m (alone or +TMZ) would improve OS vs TMZ/lomustine in recurrent EGFR-amplified GBM
sponsor_reason: Primary endpoint of OS not met (HR 0.71, p=0.06). Trended favorably but did not reach significance. Ocular dose-limiting toxicity was a key issue.
analysis: Borderline Phase 2 result (p=0.06) was the basis for proceeding to INTELLANCE-1 Phase 3. INTELLANCE-1 then completely failed. Demonstrates the danger of advancing on a near-miss Phase 2 — when you correct for multiple comparisons or wider population, near-misses tend to disappear.
notes: van den Bent et al., Neuro-Oncology 2020. Pairs with INTELLANCE-1 (newly dx Phase 3) — together show full EGFR-ADC program failure.

name: GLARIUS
drug: Bevacizumab + irinotecan
mechanism: VEGF mAb + topoisomerase I inhibitor
phase2_evidence: Phase 2 BELOB-like signal in MGMT-unmethylated subset
hypothesis: Bev + irinotecan would extend PFS vs TMZ in MGMT-unmethylated newly dx GBM (population with poor TMZ response)
notes: J Clin Oncol 2016. Verify result fields from PMID 27001569 — primary endpoint PFS@6mo was met but OS was not, consistent with anti-angiogenic class pattern.

name: CheckMate-498
drug: Nivolumab + RT (no TMZ)
mechanism: Anti-PD-1 monoclonal antibody
hypothesis: Nivolumab + RT would improve OS vs standard chemoradiation in MGMT-unmethylated newly diagnosed GBM
notes: Omuro et al., Neuro-Oncology 2023. Trial replaced TMZ with nivolumab in the MGMT-unmethylated population (rationale: TMZ less effective in unmethylated). Failed OS. Verify numeric fields from PMID.

name: CheckMate-548
drug: Nivolumab + RT + TMZ
mechanism: Anti-PD-1 monoclonal antibody added to standard chemoradiation
hypothesis: Adding nivolumab to standard chemoradiation would improve OS in MGMT-methylated newly diagnosed GBM
notes: Lim et al., Neuro-Oncology 2022. Companion to CheckMate-498 (unmethylated). Together with -498, establishes that PD-1 inhibition fails in newly diagnosed GBM regardless of MGMT status. Verify numeric fields from PMID.

name: DCVax-L
drug: Autologous tumor lysate-loaded dendritic cell vaccine
mechanism: Personalized cancer vaccine using patient's own tumor lysate to load autologous dendritic cells
hypothesis: DCVax-L added to standard care would improve OS
notes: Liau et al., JAMA Oncol 2023. METHODOLOGY CASE STUDY. Major issues: (1) primary endpoint changed from PFS to OS mid-trial; (2) all randomized patients eventually crossed over to DCVax-L, eliminating internal control; (3) comparison made against external controls from other trials, not the originally randomized placebo arm; (4) ~7 year delay between enrollment and publication. Sponsor framed as positive; methodologists generally view it as inconclusive. Excellent case study of how endpoint changes and external controls can corrupt a trial.

name: MIRAGE
drug: Marizomib + RT + TMZ
mechanism: Irreversible 20S proteasome inhibitor; CNS-penetrant
hypothesis: Adding marizomib (CNS-penetrant proteasome inhibitor) to standard chemoradiation would improve OS
notes: Roth et al., Lancet Oncol 2024. Marizomib was specifically chosen for CNS penetration (unlike bortezomib/carfilzomib). Trial halted for futility. Verify numeric fields from PMID.

name: Enzastaurin (Wick 2010)
drug: Enzastaurin oral
mechanism: Selective serine/threonine kinase inhibitor; targets PKCβ and PI3K/AKT
hypothesis: Enzastaurin would improve PFS vs lomustine in recurrent GBM
notes: Wick et al., J Clin Oncol 2010. Older trial — useful for cross-era comparison. Notable post-failure development of DGM1 biomarker (Denovo Biopharma) for re-targeting program — example of 'biomarker after the failure' pattern. Verify numeric fields from PMID.

name: REGAL
drug: Cediranib monotherapy OR cediranib + lomustine
mechanism: Pan-VEGFR tyrosine kinase inhibitor (oral)
hypothesis: Cediranib (VEGFR TKI) would improve PFS in recurrent GBM, alone or combined with lomustine
notes: Batchelor et al., J Clin Oncol 2013. Pan-VEGFR TKI failed where it was hoped to outperform bev mAb. Verify numeric fields from PMID.

name: GLOBE
drug: Ofranergene obadenovec (VB-111) + bevacizumab at progression
mechanism: Adenovirus-vectored gene therapy delivering Fas-c (chimeric death receptor) under PPE-1-3x promoter active in tumor vasculature
hypothesis: VB-111 priming followed by bev at progression would improve OS vs bev alone in recurrent GBM
notes: Cloughesy et al., J Clin Oncol 2020. Phase 2 had shown apparent benefit; Phase 3 was negative. Another instance of Phase 2 → Phase 3 effect-size collapse. Verify numeric fields from PMID.

name: RTOG 0525
drug: Dose-dense temozolomide (75-100 mg/m²/day, 21/28 days)
mechanism: DNA alkylating agent (intensified scheduling to deplete MGMT)
hypothesis: Dose-dense TMZ would deplete MGMT and improve OS, particularly in MGMT-unmethylated patients
notes: Gilbert et al., J Clin Oncol 2013. Dose-dense TMZ showed no benefit; MGMT depletion hypothesis not confirmed. Important methodological lesson: rational mechanistic hypothesis (deplete MGMT to overcome unmethylated-status resistance) didn't work in practice. Verify numeric fields from PMID.

name: Stupp 2005
drug: Temozolomide concurrent with RT, then 6 cycles adjuvant TMZ
mechanism: DNA alkylating agent (methylates O6-guanine)
hypothesis: Adding TMZ to RT would improve OS in newly diagnosed GBM
sponsor_reason: TMZ + RT extends OS vs RT alone. Established standard of care.
analysis: POSITIVE COMPARATOR — established the 'Stupp protocol' that became universal SoC. Subsequent post-hoc analysis (Hegi 2005) showed MGMT methylation predicts TMZ benefit. Importantly: median OS in MGMT-methylated patients was ~21 mo, in unmethylated ~12 mo — this becomes the historical baseline that all subsequent Phase 2 trials measured against (and overestimated their effects vs).
notes: Foundational positive trial. The 14.6-mo median OS became the historical control benchmark that subsequent failed trials' Phase 2s compared against — but as MGMT selection improved control-arm performance over time, this benchmark drifted upward, fueling the historical-control comparison failure pattern (see ACT IV, AVAglio Phase 2 evidence).

name: EF-14
drug: Tumor Treating Fields (TTFields, 200 kHz alternating electric fields) + maintenance TMZ
mechanism: Alternating electric fields disrupting mitotic spindle assembly in dividing cells
hypothesis: Adding TTFields to maintenance TMZ would extend PFS and OS
sponsor_reason: TTFields + TMZ extends OS vs TMZ alone in newly diagnosed GBM
analysis: POSITIVE COMPARATOR — second drug/device to extend OS in newly dx GBM. Useful as a contrast: a non-pharmacological intervention with a clear physical mechanism succeeded where many drug programs failed. Adherence/compliance with device wear time matters; OS benefit correlates with hours of daily use.
notes: JAMA 2017. Open-label (cannot blind a wearable device). Some controversy about magnitude of effect and the no-treatment vs sham comparison, but FDA-approved and incorporated into NCCN guidelines.

name: CeTeG
drug: Lomustine + temozolomide (combination)
mechanism: Two alkylating agents combined (lomustine = nitrosourea; TMZ = imidazotetrazine)
hypothesis: Adding lomustine to TMZ would improve OS in MGMT-methylated newly dx GBM
sponsor_reason: Combination lomustine + TMZ improves OS vs TMZ alone in MGMT-methylated newly dx GBM
analysis: POSITIVE COMPARATOR — only chemotherapy combination besides original Stupp protocol to extend OS. Small trial (n=141) with borderline p-value (0.0492) and wide CIs; not universally adopted as SoC. But the magnitude of effect (~17 mo OS extension) is striking. Open-label which may bias outcome assessment.
notes: Lancet 2019. Most controversy around small sample, open-label design, and whether the 17-month OS gain is real or partly artifact. Limited adoption outside Germany due to toxicity concerns.
